retinal_sim - Full Validation Audit Report

Generated: 2026-04-03 23:54

Scope of This Report

This audit report shows the implemented validation checks, the exact pass criteria used, the observed outcomes, the assumptions and limitations attached to each check, and the code paths that produced the result. Statements here are evidence-backed for the current proof-of-concept build, not blanket claims of full physiological completeness.

Summary

2/14 implemented validation checks failed under the current test harness

Known Limitations / Deferred Architecture Items

  • Visual-inspection checks remain labeled as visual/manual evidence, not strict quantitative proof.
  • Several validation checks use proof-of-concept simplifications documented in SCRATCHPAD.md.

Architecture Coverage Matrix

Architecture itemImplemented testCode pathStatusNote
Architecture §11e Angular subtense correctnessAngular Subtenseretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredAnalytic geometric equality check.
Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across speciesRetinal Scaling Across Speciesretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredChecks focal-length proportionality.
Architecture §11e Accommodation/defocus injectionAccommodation Defocusretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredHard-cutoff accommodation model.
Architecture §11e Distance-dependent receptor samplingDistance-Dependent Receptor Samplingretinal_sim/pipeline.pycoveredApproximate inverse-square scaling.
Architecture §11a Metamer preservationMetamer Preservationretinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.pypartially coveredUses practical proxy, not a formal metamer dataset.
Architecture §11a Roundtrip consistencyRGB Round-Tripretinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.pycoveredApproximate CIE path with explicit tolerance.
Architecture §11b Diffraction-limited resolutionMTF vs Diffraction Limitretinal_sim/optical/psf.pypartially coveredPSF-width proxy rather than full closed-form MTF.
Architecture §11b PSF energy conservationPSF Energy Conservationretinal_sim/optical/psf.pycoveredFloat64 normalization check.
Architecture §11c Snellen acuity predictionSnellen Acuityretinal_sim/validation/acuity.pycoveredOrdering plus published-range checks.
Architecture §11c Dichromat confusion axisDichromat Confusionretinal_sim/validation/dichromat.pycoveredHuman vs dog discriminability comparison.
Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limitNyquist Samplingretinal_sim/retina/mosaic.pypartially coveredUses spacing proxy instead of full cpd derivation.
Architecture §11c Receptor count validationReceptor Countretinal_sim/retina/mosaic.pypartially coveredNon-zero count check is weaker than literature calibration.
Architecture §11d Known visual deficit reproductionColor Deficit Reproductionretinal_sim/pipeline.pycoveredEnd-to-end species comparison.
Architecture §11d Resolution gradientResolution Gradientretinal_sim/output/reconstruction.pypartially coveredVisual/manual evidence with variance context.

Test Details, Evidence, and Provenance

Angular Subtense PASS

Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Angular subtense correctness
Expected outcome
2*arctan(w/(2d))
Observed outcome
w=0.2m, d=1.0m → 11.4212° (expected 11.4212°) ✓ w=1.0m, d=10.0m → 5.7248° (expected 5.7248°) ✓ w=0.3m, d=6.0m → 2.8642° (expected 2.8642°) ✓
Tolerance
0.01
Pass criterion
Absolute angular error < 0.01 degrees for every case.
Inputs summary
Three analytic scene width/distance cases using the human optical model.
Method
Compare computed angular width against the closed-form geometric equation.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/report.py
Assumptions
  • Exact 2*arctan geometry is the source of truth.
  • Human optical focal length is used only to build SceneDescription.
Limitations
  • Checks representative cases, not exhaustive parameter sweeps.
Artifacts
  • Bar chart: expected vs computed angular width
w=0.2m, d=1.0m → 11.4212° (expected 11.4212°) ✓
w=1.0m, d=10.0m → 5.7248° (expected 5.7248°) ✓
w=0.3m, d=6.0m → 2.8642° (expected 2.8642°) ✓

Retinal Scaling Across Species PASS

Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across species
Expected outcome
retinal_width ∝ focal_length
Observed outcome
human: retinal_w=1.1150mm, focal=22.3mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓ dog: retinal_w=0.8500mm, focal=17.0mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓ cat: retinal_w=0.9250mm, focal=18.5mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
Tolerance
0.01
Pass criterion
Species ratios agree within 1% relative error.
Inputs summary
One shared physical scene evaluated for human, dog, and cat species configs.
Method
Compare retinal_width_mm / focal_length_mm across species.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
Assumptions
  • Retinal image width should scale with focal length when angular size is fixed.
Limitations
  • Only validates proportionality, not downstream sampling behavior.
Artifacts
  • Species focal-length bar chart
  • Species retinal-width bar chart
human: retinal_w=1.1150mm, focal=22.3mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
dog: retinal_w=0.8500mm, focal=17.0mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
cat: retinal_w=0.9250mm, focal=18.5mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓

Accommodation Defocus PASS

Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §0 Accommodation demand and residual defocus
Expected outcome
max(0, demand - max_accommodation)
Observed outcome
human: demand=4.0D, max_acc=10.0D, residual=0.00D (expected 0.00D) ✓ dog: demand=4.0D, max_acc=2.5D, residual=1.50D (expected 1.50D) ✓ cat: demand=4.0D, max_acc=3.0D, residual=1.00D (expected 1.00D) ✓
Tolerance
0.1
Pass criterion
Residual defocus matches expected value within 0.1 diopters.
Inputs summary
Near-view scene at 0.25 m for human, dog, and cat.
Method
Compare computed residual defocus against max(0, demand - max_accommodation).
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
Assumptions
  • Accommodation is a hard cutoff.
  • Max accommodation values are fixed PoC constants.
Limitations
  • No lead/lag accommodation response model is applied.
Artifacts
  • Expected vs computed residual defocus bars
human: demand=4.0D, max_acc=10.0D, residual=0.00D (expected 0.00D) ✓
dog: demand=4.0D, max_acc=2.5D, residual=1.50D (expected 1.50D) ✓
cat: demand=4.0D, max_acc=3.0D, residual=1.00D (expected 1.00D) ✓

Distance-Dependent Receptor Sampling FAIL

Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Distance-dependent receptor sampling
Expected outcome
~1/d² scaling
Observed outcome
d=1m → 191176 receptors d=5m → 191176 receptors d=10m → 191176 receptors count ratio (d=1.0m/d=10.0m): 1.0 vs expected 100.0 (rel err 0.99) ✗
Tolerance
0.5
Pass criterion
Far/near receptor-count scaling stays within 50% relative error of ideal 1/d^2.
Inputs summary
Constant-size grayscale object at 1 m, 5 m, and 10 m using the human simulator.
Method
Measure receptor counts covered by the scene at multiple viewing distances and compare against ideal inverse-square scaling.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/pipeline.py
  • retinal_sim/retina/mosaic.py
Assumptions
  • 1/d^2 scaling is approximate because density gradients remain in play.
Limitations
  • Tolerance is intentionally generous due to non-uniform mosaic density.
Artifacts
  • Distance vs receptor count plot
  • Log-log ideal vs measured scaling plot
d=1m → 191176 receptors
d=5m → 191176 receptors
d=10m → 191176 receptors
count ratio (d=1.0m/d=10.0m): 1.0 vs expected 100.0 (rel err 0.99) ✗

Metamer Preservation PASS

Stage
spectral
Architecture reference
Architecture §11a Metamer preservation
Expected outcome
Similar cone catches for near-metameric inputs
Observed outcome
S_cone: 16.1985 vs 16.1560 (rel_diff=0.0026) ✓ M_cone: 25.8705 vs 25.3594 (rel_diff=0.0198) ✓ L_cone: 27.7564 vs 27.3726 (rel_diff=0.0138) ✓ rod: 23.8551 vs 23.4635 (rel_diff=0.0164) ✓
Tolerance
0.1
Pass criterion
Relative cone-catch difference < 10% for each receptor class.
Inputs summary
Two near-gray RGB triplets upsampled to spectra and integrated against human receptor sensitivities.
Method
Compare integrated cone catches from two near-metameric inputs.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.py
  • retinal_sim/retina/opsin.py
Assumptions
  • Near-metameric RGBs are used as a practical proxy instead of a published metamer dataset.
Limitations
  • This is a practical approximation, not a formal metamer library benchmark.
Artifacts
  • Upsampled spectra plot
  • Cone-catch comparison bars
S_cone: 16.1985 vs 16.1560 (rel_diff=0.0026) ✓
M_cone: 25.8705 vs 25.3594 (rel_diff=0.0198) ✓
L_cone: 27.7564 vs 27.3726 (rel_diff=0.0138) ✓
rod: 23.8551 vs 23.4635 (rel_diff=0.0164) ✓

RGB Round-Trip FAIL

Stage
spectral
Architecture reference
Architecture §11a Roundtrip consistency
Expected outcome
RMSE < 15.0 for all colours
Observed outcome
Max RMSE = 73.67
Tolerance
15.0
Pass criterion
RMSE < 15 8-bit counts for all tested colors.
Inputs summary
Eight canonical sRGB colors passed through spectral upsampling and approximate CIE XYZ reconstruction.
Method
Reconstruct RGB via approximate XYZ integration and compute per-color RMSE.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/report.py
Assumptions
  • Fallback CIE observer curves may be approximate.
  • D65 is simplified in this report implementation.
Limitations
  • Tolerance is generous because the reconstruction path is approximate and validation-oriented.
Artifacts
  • RMSE bar chart
  • Input vs reconstructed color swatches
Red: in=[255, 0, 0] out=[255,100,0] RMSE=57.69 ✗
Green: in=[0, 255, 0] out=[125,232,0] RMSE=73.67 ✗
Blue: in=[0, 0, 255] out=[0,45,234] RMSE=28.50 ✗
Yellow: in=[255, 255, 0] out=[255,247,0] RMSE=4.65 ✓
Cyan: in=[0, 255, 255] out=[106,235,228] RMSE=64.19 ✗
Magenta: in=[255, 0, 255] out=[255,109,234] RMSE=64.09 ✗
White: in=[255, 255, 255] out=[255,250,229] RMSE=15.15 ✗
Gray: in=[128, 128, 128] out=[141,125,114] RMSE=10.78 ✓

MTF vs Diffraction Limit PASS

Stage
optical
Architecture reference
Architecture §11b Diffraction-limited resolution
Expected outcome
PSF width increases with wavelength
Observed outcome
FWHMs: [np.int64(2), np.int64(2), np.int64(4), np.int64(4)]
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Measured FWHM is non-decreasing with wavelength.
Inputs summary
Human Gaussian PSF kernels at 400, 500, 600, and 700 nm.
Method
Measure PSF profile widths across wavelength and verify monotonic broadening.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/optical/psf.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
Assumptions
  • Current PoC uses Gaussian PSF behavior as the optical reference.
Limitations
  • This does not compute a full empirical MTF curve against the closed-form diffraction equation.
Artifacts
  • PSF cross-section plot
  • PSF kernel montage
λ=400nm → FWHM=2 px
λ=500nm → FWHM=2 px
λ=600nm → FWHM=4 px
λ=700nm → FWHM=4 px

PSF Energy Conservation PASS

Stage
optical
Architecture reference
Architecture §11b PSF energy conservation
Expected outcome
|sum(PSF) - 1.0| < 1e-6
Observed outcome
human: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓ dog: max |sum-1| = 4.44e-16 ✓ cat: max |sum-1| = 4.44e-16 ✓
Tolerance
1e-06
Pass criterion
Maximum |sum - 1.0| < 1e-6.
Inputs summary
Gaussian PSF kernels across the canonical wavelength grid for all three species.
Method
Sum each PSF kernel and measure max absolute deviation from 1.0.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/optical/psf.py
Assumptions
  • Kernel normalization in float64 is the source of truth for this check.
Limitations
  • Only validates discrete kernel normalization, not broader optical energy accounting.
Artifacts
  • Log-scale wavelength vs normalization error plot
human: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓
dog: max |sum-1| = 4.44e-16 ✓
cat: max |sum-1| = 4.44e-16 ✓

Snellen Acuity PASS

Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Snellen acuity prediction
Expected outcome
human < dog ≈ cat; within published ranges
Observed outcome
human=2.0′, dog=8.0′, cat=8.0′
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Each species falls within its target range and human acuity is best.
Inputs summary
Species-specific acuity prediction using Snellen E orientation discriminability.
Method
Predict minimum resolvable angular size for each species and compare with expected ranges and ordering.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/validation/acuity.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/snellen.py
Assumptions
  • Published acuity ranges are broad PoC acceptance bands.
  • Letter-region masking and Pearson correlation distance are required.
Limitations
  • Cat predictions can be seed-sensitive near threshold.
Artifacts
  • Snellen stimulus examples
  • Predicted acuity bar chart
human: predicted 2.0 arcmin (expected 1.0–5.0) ✓
dog: predicted 8.0 arcmin (expected 3.0–20.0) ✓
cat: predicted 8.0 arcmin (expected 3.0–20.0) ✓
Ordering human ≤ dog ≤ cat: human=2.0, dog=8.0, cat=8.0 ✓

Dichromat Confusion PASS

Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Dichromat confusion axis
Expected outcome
D_human > D_dog
Observed outcome
D_human=0.0374, D_dog=0.0196
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Human discriminability exceeds dog discriminability.
Inputs summary
Dog confusion pair rendered as an Ishihara-like dot pattern and scored for human vs dog.
Method
Compute discriminability for the same color pair under trichromat and dichromat models.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/validation/dichromat.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/ishihara.py
Assumptions
  • Stimulus scaling is applied to keep Naka-Rushton responses out of saturation.
Limitations
  • With low seed counts, noise can inflate small dog discriminability values.
Artifacts
  • Pattern image
  • Ground-truth mask
  • Human vs dog discriminability bars
Confusion pair: fg=[226, 186, 6], bg=[44, 218, 44]
Human D = 0.0374
Dog D = 0.0196

Nyquist Sampling PASS

Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limit
Expected outcome
Spacing ≤ PSF FWHM
Observed outcome
human: mean NN spacing=1.1µm, PSF FWHM(550nm)=4.0µm, Nyquist (spacing ≤ FWHM): ✓ dog: mean NN spacing=1.0µm, PSF FWHM(550nm)=2.3µm, Nyquist (spacing ≤ FWHM): ✓ cat: mean NN spacing=0.9µm, PSF FWHM(550nm)=5.0µm, Nyquist (spacing ≤ FWHM): ✓
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Mean nearest-neighbor spacing is no larger than the PSF FWHM proxy.
Inputs summary
Generated mosaics for all species, compared against PSF width at 550 nm.
Method
Estimate nearest-neighbor receptor spacing and compare against PSF FWHM bandwidth proxy.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/retina/mosaic.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
Assumptions
  • Mean nearest-neighbor spacing is used as the sampling proxy.
Limitations
  • Uses a simplified spacing-vs-bandwidth criterion rather than full cpd analysis.
Artifacts
